mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate signals a tightening monetary policy that raises discount rates, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in >60% of instances despite rising FEDFUNDS, or Rank IC <0.03 over 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: If CPIAUCSL exceeds 310, it signals elevated inflation expectations, prompting the Fed to raise rates, which compresses equity valuations and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are positive in >60% of instances when CPIAUCSL >310, or IC <0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL indicates a tightening cycle amid inflation, creating uncertainty that drives negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are positive in >60% of instances when both FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL rise, or IC <0.03.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, rising FEDFUNDS signals higher discount rates, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are positive in >60% of tightening periods, or IC <0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) indicates strong corporate earnings growth, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative in >60% of INDPRO rising periods, or IC <0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in PAYEMS above 150k signals robust job market growth, boosting consumer spending and driving positive S&P 500 returns over 5 days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative in >60% of PAYEMS >150k instances, or IC <0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) indicate increased demand for construction, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative in >60% of HOUST rising periods, or IC <0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth (GDPC1) signals robust economic expansion, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative in >60% of GDPC1 growth periods, or IC <0.03.
